title: Ten years after Brexit, Britons want closer ties to the EU
subhead: According to a survey by the European Council on Foreign Relations, British voters, disappointed by the 'negative impact' of leaving the EU, stand ready to accept the restoration of freedom of movement between the UK and Europe.

title: Alan Greenspan, former Fed chair and champion of market self-regulation, dies 100
subhead: The American economist, who led the US central bank for nearly two decades, died Monday, June 22, at the age of 100.

title: 250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subhead: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.

title: 'Twisted Face,' the French Gestapo collaborator who arrested historian and resistance fighter Marc Bloch
subhead: France André, known as 'Twisted Face,' was a staunch French collaborator who led a gang of nearly 100 people in the Lyon area. They spread terror through thefts, arrests, torture and murders, all committed for personal gain and to serve the Nazis. Among his many victims was Marc Bloch, a Jewish historian and resistance fighter who will be inducted into the Panthéon on June 23.

title: Free parties in France come under increasing surveillance
subhead: Under an upcoming bill, which is set to be debated in the Assemblée Nationale on Monday, prison sentences would apply not only to the organizers of these self-managed techno parties, but also to their attendees. Protest parties have taken place across France in response to this crackdown.
